_comment: BEST-QUALITY UNSTRUCTURED flip of unstructured_gmsh/paper_comparison_irregular.json. ONLY difference: mesh_algorithm=6 (gmsh Frontal-Delaunay) instead of the default 5 (plain Delaunay). Motivation (2026-07-19): a mesh-quality screen showed Frontal-Delaunay produces near-equilateral triangles (gamma quality mean 0.998, min 0.87, sliver tail eliminated) vs plain Delaunay (mean 0.949, min 0.65) with FEWER cells; and the settled Cocquet finding is that the O(h^2) slope for the Taylor-Hood (Galerkin P2/P1) row is recovered on ANY unstructured mesh (the structured Cartesian mesh locks in the outlet-corner singularity). This variant tests whether the highest-quality isotropic unstructured mesh ALSO tightens the constant/magnitude, per the 3D-MMS lesson that Delaunay->Frontal fixes the element-quality tail. comparison_runs orders the Cocquet Galerkin P2/P1 (Taylor-Hood) FIRST so its convergence + interpolation-error reference stream before the VMS rows. Re=500, c_in=0.5, transverse porosity; N_ref=200 self-reference over coarse N in {10,20,40,80,100}. eps_val/physical_epsilon=1e-7 (paper eta). Run: julia --project=../../.. run_convergence.jl data/unstructured_frontal/paper_comparison_frontal.json ; python plot_convergence.py unstructured_frontal
